input.background: We have established that the body weights of 20-29-year-old U.S. males have σ=40 pounds.
input.question: How many observations do we need in order to estimate mean body weight μ with 95% confidence and a margin of error of plus or minus 10 pounds?

output.method: 达到指定概率所需的最小样本量
output.answer: Recall that for 95% confidence, z_{1-\frac{9}{2}}=z_{1-\frac{\beta}{2}}=z_{.975}=1.96 (from Table B). Therefore, the required sample size is n=\left(z_1-\frac{\sigma}{m}\right)^2= \left(1.96 \cdot \frac{40}{10}\right)^2=61.4. Round this up to 62.

sigma.description: The standard deviation of body weights for 20-29-year-old U.S. males.

confidence_level.description: The confidence level for estimating the mean body weight.

margin_of_error.description: The desired margin of error for the estimate of the mean body weight.

mean_body_weight.description: The mean body weight of 20-29-year-old U.S. males to be estimated.
